post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 306 (Newbie Game 113)

Important Rules:
1.6 Do not edit or delete posts
1.2 Do not post during night
1.7 Do not post if you are dead or if you are not in the game
1.4 Do not talk about anything related to this game outside this thread
1.1 Read all the rules!

All players should also pay additional attention to rules 2.9 and 2.10; they describe an experimental deadline/majority hybrid elimination procedure that will be introduced in this game.

Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.5: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.9: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#00FF00), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.10: The confirm rule for this game will be detailed in post #2.
  • Voting/Elimination Procedures
    • 2.1: Post votes in code tags in the format 
      [code]Vote: <insert player here>[/‎code]

      Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. A proper vote looks like 
      Vote: fdas
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang/elimination to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post 
      Vote: No Hang
      or 
      Vote: No Elimination
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format 
      Vote: Unvote

      An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no player has been hanged, the amount of votes required for a Hang or a No Hang will be reduced by 1. The vote threshold will reduce by 1 vote every 24 hours after this. For example, if there are 8 players alive in the game, 5 votes will be required to hang someone and 4 votes are required for a no hang. Once the deadline passes, 4 votes will be sufficient to hang someone and 3 votes are sufficient for a no hang. 24 hours after the deadline, 3 votes will be sufficient to hang someone and 2 votes are sufficient for a no hang.
    • 2.10: If the vote threshold is decreased and two or more options have both reached the threshold required, then the game will enter tiebreak. During tiebreak, votes can continue to be changed. If at any point in time, only one option has enough votes to meet the threshold required for the option to be enacted, day will immediately end. Or, if any option gains votes and now exceeds the vote threshold by an amount of votes strictly greater than the amount of votes any other option exceeds their vote threshold by, that option will win and day will immediately end. If 24 hours have passed since tiebreak has begun, and the votes are still deadlocked, there will be no hang.
    • 2.11: After an option has won the vote, it will become twilight and players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 168 hours (1 week). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 168 hours has elapsed.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 72 hours after night begins.
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.11: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I scannose am the moderator of Mafia 306
    • 5.2: This orange color (#ff9a00) is my mod color. Do not post in anything too similar.
    • 5.3: This light blue-ish color (#9fc5e8) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.4: This light green-ish color (#b6d7a8) is used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
This game uses the Matrix6 setup, a Semi-Open setup.

The setup will be created by picking all three roles in a random row or column and adding 1 Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies. Therefore the six possible setups are as follows
Setup 1: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 Town 1-Shot Buleetproof, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
Setup 2: 1 Town Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
Setup 3: 1 Town Tracker, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
Setup 4: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
Setup 5: 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
Setup 6: 1 Town Tracker, 1 Town 1-Shot Bulletproof, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons

After the specific setup is selected, the nine roles will be randomly distributed to players.

There will additionally be a Town Mentor who advises the town. They cannot vote and they cannot be eliminated from the game. Their identity is known to all players. There is also a Mafia Mentor who will advise the mafia players in their private chat.

Role PMs
Quote:
Welcome, player, to Mafia Game 306.

You are a snow golem, Vanilla Townie. A lifeform created from inanimate objects, you live with the sole purpose of wandering around and pelting chickens with snowballs. You have no power other than your voice and your vote.

Did you know that the first snowman was created in the 14th century? You have no idea how many years you have been alive for, but no matter what time in the year, the season always seems to be winter. The pine trees are always covered by snow, and you've never seen a sprout pop out from the ground.

Did you know that snowmen were used to (unsuccessfully) guard a settlement in 1690? This would be the case once again, when one day you were informed of the existence of intruders in your community. You heard that imposters have discovered a crystal that prevents the season from switching in your region, and they will destroy this crystal if they aren't stopped.

Did you know that snow will melt away when spring comes?

You win when all members of the raindrops (Mafia) have been eliminated from the game. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Quote:
Welcome, player, to Mafia Game 306.

You are Poley the polar bear, Town Cop. You used to be a police officer at the Lucella Police Department, but one day you were kidnapped and disappeared offstage soon after. No one knows your true whereabouts nowadays, except a couple of rumors circulating around that you are very coolio and have made friends with a school bus. Each night, you may choose a player and using your old skills, learning if they are aligned with the snowflakes (Town) or the raindrops (Mafia).

You win when all members of the raindrops (Mafia) have been eliminated from the game. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Quote:
Welcome, player, to Mafia Game 306.

You are Kaeya, Town Jailkeeper. You originally resided within the realm of Genshin Impact and fdas games, but after losing a thunderdome to a vig claim who was outed twice, you decided to take refuge here. Each night, you may afflict someone with Cryo and freeze them; they will be protected if they are attacked by the mafia, but they will be unable to take any night actions.

You win when all members of the raindrops (Mafia) have been eliminated from the game. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Quote:
Welcome, player, to Mafia Game 306.

You are Kevin McCallister, Town Tracker. You are home alone after your parents and siblings went on a vacation without you, but you quickly realize your freedom allows you to do some very funny things you've always wanted to do for a long time. Each night, you may prank call another player, playing TV clips to sound like their relative, and ask them who they plan on visiting that night.

You win when all members of the raindrops (Mafia) have been eliminated from the game. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Quote:
Welcome, player, to Mafia Game 306.

You are Grillby, Town Doctor. Despite your decision to relocate your restaurant, you opted to move to a cold biome similar to your original home, and this small snowy settlement is perfect for you. Each night, you may deliver a cup of handmade (literally) hot coffee to another player, protecting them if they are attacked by the mafia. You may choose to protect yourself, but not on any two consecutive nights.

You win when all members of the raindrops (Mafia) have been eliminated from the game. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Quote:
Welcome, player, to Mafia Game 306.

You are Shih Tzu, 1-shot Bulletproof Townie. A dog breed originating from the Tibet regions, you are accustomed to living in cold climates.

As we all know, a true Shih Tzu never dies. On the first time you are attacked by the mafia, your double layer of fur coats will protect you from dying. However, you will not be notified of this effect taking place, and you will still die if you are attacked a second time.

You win when all members of the raindrops (Mafia) have been eliminated from the game. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Quote:
Welcome, player, to Mafia Game 306.

You are a creaking, Mafia Goon. You belong to the pale forest, which unfortunately has been covered by years and years worth of snow. The only way to restore your habitat is through a stealth mission, and your character design is perfect for that.

You share a factional private chat with your partner player (flavorname, role), which the link to will be in the following post. Each night, either you or your partner may choose to attack another player, eliminating them from the game.

You win when the living members of your faction reaches parity with the members of the snowflakes (Town). Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Quote:
Welcome, player, to Mafia Game 306.

You are AP Physics C: Electromagnetism, Mafia Roleblocker. You represent the academic stress that comes with spring, the reason scan didn't get straight 5s on his ap exams, and the knowledge the town needs to prevent their machine from being hijacked.

You share a factional private chat with your partner player (creaking, Mafia Goon), which the link to will be in the following post. Each night, either you or your partner may choose to attack another player, eliminating them from the game.

Each night, you may additionally choose a player and force them to stay up until 3am to prepare for their upcoming test, which would prevent them from taking any night actions.

You win when the living members of your faction reaches parity with the members of the snowflakes (Town). Play towards your win condition, and good luck!



Alive (6)
1. the_count_of_monte_cristo
3. NoodlesSKY galaxy1220
5. mxsail
7. violetmx12
8. vietaformula810
9. awesomeming327.

Dead (3)
2. stjwyl
6. sdmd
4. JohannIsBach


notable stuff
 


Do not post until this line is crossed out.
Current phase: Day 3
Deadline: September 21, 6pm est

post_rendered:
Hi, I am going to be your Town mentor for this game. My role here is to provide guidance and help for players to improve their play. Feel free to ask me questions about how to play the game and anything you are confused about. Though I am the Town mentor, mafia players can ask me questions too just like the town players then, since I do not know who is town and who is mafia in this game.

In this game, I will
Answer questions about game mechanics as well as any other general questions about the game.
Give advice on how you can improve your play.
Answer questions on what you ought to be doing at the moment.
Occasionally prod players and encourage them to give their thoughts and reads on some or all players.
Debate and discuss with players about their posts. If you have no one to talk to, you can always try talking to me.
Provide mechanical analysis of night actions and claims, up to and including outright solving the game if it can be mechanically solved.
Quote posts from the game and reference past events in order to bring them back to the attention of the town if it has been forgotten or overlooked. These quotes may appear incriminating to the people involved, but I will try to refrain from directly presenting my own conclusions, instead I will direct you to make deductions based on the material.

In this game, I will not
Provide subjective reads on players or actively scumhunt, unless it falls into one of the cases outlined above.
Encourage players to sheep my opinions without thinking for themselves.

Do not expect me to solve the game for you, unless it is a case where I believe the truth should be obvious based solely on public information.


Some other things to keep in mind:
Do not claim your role without a good reason to. Even if you feel like you are under pressure and think things are doomed for you, unless it is close to the deadline and most of the players are asking you to claim. If you have any doubts about whether you should claim, you can ask me. You may also claim if you are a cop with a guilty check or a tracker who has discovered a player visiting someone who died that night. In these cases, the decision to claim is more up to your discretion, as you cannot publicly ask whether to claim without revealing your role. If it is day 2 and you have a guilty check, then you can choose to not claim if you are confident that you will not die and you believe the players see you as towny enough that they will believe your claim. You may choose to claim if you either suspect you may be targeted by the mafia or if you do not feel confident that you have enough towncred for your claim to be believed.

Lastly, everyone should be aware of the unique hang mechanics in this game. A player is hanged upon reaching a majority of the votes, but if no player reaches a majority by the deadline, the threshold required to hang someone will decrease by 1 and will continue decreasing every 24 hours. Be sure to remain active throughout the game and do not wait until the deadline to start putting down votes. You should be actively working on deciding on a hang throughout the day, don't procrastinate until the end!

After some more thought, I see three possibilities:

1. You are town trying to elicit a reaction, allowing you to get a better read on me.
2. You are scum trying to pocket me.
3. You have not read your role PM and are just fooling around.

1. Eliciting a reaction
Thinking from your perspective (assuming your town), quickly marking a player off as town and seeing how, and if they react can give you some basic insight. A scum player quickly marked off as town would probably go "Oh great, someone townreads me. Nothing to worry about!", While town players that are trying to gather information and piece the game together won't just reluctantly shrug that off, especially this early with so little information.

2. Early game pocket
You are a pretty competent player and rashly marking two players off as town without evidence or reasoning is obviously scummy; I can't really see a scum violet doing that, rather they would go much deeper and provide adequate reasoning that makes them look towny while forwarding there scum agenda (by pocketing players).

3. Trolling
STOP TROLLING OR I'M GONNA CRASH OUT!

post_rendered:
hi!
i will keep things brief - i am one of your more experienced players, but i'm bad at teaching mafia so refer to fdas if you want to learn something or ask for better clarification because that's what he's here for
what i am here to do in this game is to keep things on track and to solve, so the important things for you all to know about that is that i don't do cases, i will be posting a lot of raw reads, i will be putting largely all of my thoughts down in thread, i will be in thread a bit more than you might think just to observe and get a feel for the game and where it's headed, and if i pull rank on something it's probably good to listen

probably my only tidbit of advice while typing this up is that mafia is a game of communication, and if you're a villager a good mindset to have is that you're always actively collaborating to figure out who you trust and who you don't, and put simply being stubborn or bullish or tunneled or any combinations of those things won't be getting us anywhere
so be willing to reevaluate and talk to people!
mafia is also a much less serious game than you might think, and there's often no solid rhyme or reason or logic and never a secret-little-hidden-100%-of-the-time tell, so be willing to adapt and think outside the box

lastly, it would also be good for you all to understand the newfangled voting system that fdas has implemented here because it's a little different than what some of you may know or be familiar with

These are things that sound good in the abstract, but in practice often times agenda can only be determined by looking at how several posts fit together and how they are positioned relative to the state of discussion at the time. A post can be okay at one point in the game but making that exact same post at a different point in time would not be okay. Or maybe a post by itself is fine but combined with several other posts show an agenda. Posts can't be analyzed in isolation, you have to look at them in the broader context.

post_rendered:
Inital Readlist, D1

Violetmx12:
Violet has both quality and quantity, posting a lot and generating discussion, which is generally towny. More telling, however, was that Violet was willing to lock horns with me and fully embody our prolonged bickering, townleaning them. Scum usually avoid prolonged arguments since this puts them in the spotlight and focuses most of the game on their interactions. Moderate Townlean.

Awesomeming327:
Let’s compare Ming to a similarly skilled player, Violet. For starters, they’ve done astronomically less - The only thing they have are two unexplained “not reads”: “eugh” on me and town on noodles. This isn’t necessarily scummy, but it isn’t what I expected from an experienced player at this phase, who I think should be participating more.
Turning our attention to interactions, things become sketchy. Ming claims “Noodles is towny” despite not remembering any of Noodle’s posts. Then they read me as “eugh” and justified it as “ragebaiting” when questioned.
I wonder if Ming genuinely read Noodles. It feels like TMI, given how easily Ming marked noodles as town. Reading someone and immediately proceeding to “forget all their posts” feels like a contradiction. Mild Scumlean. It’s too early to fully scumcore someone. (unless they do something really stup!d, which we will see later)

Stjwyl:
The confusion about game mechanics and terminology appears genuine, given that more experienced players were also somewhat confused. Stjwyl attempts to mediate between me and Violet, and their admission of fault when they misunderstood timing implicates an open-minded townie. Mild Townlean. (townreads are weak)

JohannIsBach:
Johann was somewhat late to the discussion, which is okay on its own, but the exact timings of their posts are very concerning. He started by lurking while everyone else was posting (I saw him viewing) and continued to do so throughout the day. While everyone else was getting into the groove, introducing themselves, and so on. He was sitting on the sidelines, ominously viewing. Three people prompted him independently… Only for a cheap, poorly done RQS.
That was bad enough on its own, but then, as soon as the first non-troll vote landed, he instantly jumped on the wagon out of nowhere, completely bandwagoning the wagoner. Not only is this post and timing very opportunistic, but it may also serve as a cleverly disguised prod dodge. His RQS post was made August 24, 2:08 PM, while his wagoning post was made August 25, 9:14 PM. Although these posts are only 27 hours apart (prod timers are 48 hours), it has now been over 48 hours since his RQS posts, suggesting this post may have been tactically timed to split the 48-hour chunk in two. Full Scumread. PoE

The_count_of_monte_cristo:
Count’s observations are well-reasoned and relevant, showing a genuine effort to solve. They noted my potential OMGUS on Ming and questioned Stjwyl's "confusion." Given that thier a newer player, it's unlikely they're at the stage of deep or powerwolfing, implicating town. Moderate townlean

NoodlesSKY:
Very difficult to read. Complete fluffposting, no real effort or contribution. Not inherently scummy for a newbie, but they need to be pressured and questioned to generate content. Nullread

Vietaformula810:
Vieta is steering the discussion in a productive direction. I recognize that their approach differs from mine, and I see how they prioritize mentoring newcomers by encouraging them to develop their own reads. Because most highly experienced players do this in newbie games, I evaluate it as NAI. However, I’m open to reevaluating my perspective since I do not fully understand the current site meta. Nullread

Sdmd:
Similar to Noodles, but with experience. The lack of activity is mildly scummy when combined with experience. I’ll excuse them for having a legitimate reason for their absence. Nullread

Summary

Most Likely Scumteam:
Johann/Ming

Other potential Scumteams:
Johann/Stjwyl (will explain later) - low probability

Towncore:
Violet, Count

Unknown/Mild reads:
Vieta, Sdmd, Noodles

Course Of Action
There's still a lot of time left in D1, so I'll pressure vote Noodles.
Vote: NoodlesSKY

Noodles, can we please get a readlist? (perhaps similar to this one)

Vietaformual810
Many people have said this post has TMI for varying reasons:
vietaformula810 wrote:
there's honestly not much i can do for you all because a lot of the wind is taken out when it becomes a solving game with zero impact
i will try to talk this out a little more for your sakes's though

the team is either exactly johann/violet with a fakeclaim in consideration but it's very unlikely or something else which is much more likely
thread dynamic implies a full newb team and ming and violet both have minor points in, so working on the thought line of {count, stj, noodles, mx, sdmd} with removal of johann for a semi-believable cop claim
influencing these reads is that it happens normally that a newb team will lean towards conventional play and something clean which essentially reduces noodles out of the equation and minorly stj
so you're working out {count, mx, sdmd} + {stj}

that is something good for you all to think about for the time being

I think TMI is fundamentally links tied to tightly. So let’s look at the evidence -> reasoning -> implications of this post:

Missing evidence? -> Fakeclaim -> Johann/Violet solve.
Thread dynamic -> No reasoning? -> Full newbie team.

The first one stands out because it doesn’t really make sense and lacks evidence. As Violet said:
violetmx12 wrote:
.
hmmyes
pressure my scumpartner into fake claiming lmao

Why would Violet bus a completely incompetent teammate then ask them to fake claim D1? At least in Matrix6, if not in LyLo, fake claiming for a 1:1 town/scum only harms scum, except in extreme circumstances.

For the second line of thinking, I think the connections are too tightly linked. Vieta completely rules out Ming and Violet using just one tool, thread dynamics. This isn’t meant to discredit this as a scumhunting method, but ultimately, it’s just a tool. Not a read. I also think this post is a bit too assertive. A townie tone usually includes lots of “I think” or “could be,” etc. When a post lacks these, it can also indicate TMI. Town needs to solve the game and naturally uses these indicators, whereas scum already knows the answer and shows it more subtly in their tone. You’ll notice this often in 303, where, after reflection, I recognized some of these subtleties in my own posts. One more thing I dislike about Vieta is how they dodged the TMI accusations and subtly shifted the thread away from it.
vietaformula810 wrote:
even as a baseline you'd expect tmi to be doing something more than a list of four players lol

This post kind of evades directly engaging the point while shrugging it off jokingly. Sure, one could argue that the first half of the sentence responds to the point, but it isn’t anything significant and doesn’t invite further discussion, which is unusual for Vieta. More importantly, removing half the playerlist from PoE and then trying to say that it isn’t significant doesn’t really make sense to me.

vietaformula810 wrote:
hi!
i will keep things brief - i am one of your more experienced players, but i'm bad at teaching mafia so refer to fdas if you want to learn something or ask for better clarification because that's what he's here for
what i am here to do in this game is to keep things on track and to solve, so the important things for you all to know about that is that i don't do cases, i will be posting a lot of raw reads, i will be putting largely all of my thoughts down in thread, i will be in thread a bit more than you might think just to observe and get a feel for the game and where it's headed, and if i pull rank on something it's probably good to listen

probably my only tidbit of advice while typing this up is that mafia is a game of communication, and if you're a villager a good mindset to have is that you're always actively collaborating to figure out who you trust and who you don't, and put simply being stubborn or bullish or tunneled or any combinations of those things won't be getting us anywhere
so be willing to reevaluate and talk to people!
mafia is also a much less serious game than you might think, and there's often no solid rhyme or reason or logic and never a secret-little-hidden-100%-of-the-time tell, so be willing to adapt and think outside the box

lastly, it would also be good for you all to understand the newfangled voting system that fdas has implemented here because it's a little different than what some of you may know or be familiar with

Frankly, Johann living and getting an actual result is quite bizarre, which is why I want to rule out the possibility that they are fakeclaiming.
vietaformula810 wrote:
this is stunning doc play

I wouldn't say the doc play is particularly stunning. All they did was protect the cop claim.

As for why we know that mafia tried to shoot the cop, we know the mafia did not roleblock the cop. The mafia mentor would not have allowed mafia to play completely incompetently, so the only reason for mafia not to roleblock the cop is if they were shooting the cop and planned on roleblocking the doctor.

Thus, the only way we could have gotten this chain of events is if the sole remaining mafia member believed they had positively identified the doctor and thought they could roleblock them and was counting on this fancy play to give them a chance at winning. This would mean that this mafia player is someone who is confident enough in themselves to make this gamble and not roleblock the cop. I'll leave it as an exercise for the rest of you to try to determine who among the players might have such confidence in their deductions.

the important things to take away from these posts is that stj, looking for stances to take in thread, prefers to attack lhf and disengage from things he feels can be risky for his alignment. that seems pretty obvious but stj is not a bold wolf who will readily townside; it's more common that he'll take tmi and wolfside.

2. analysis

With a cop dead, the only possible setups are Cop + Doctor + Mafia Roleblocker or Cop with no Doctor or Mafia Roleblocker. A doctor claim is not verifiable because we do not know which setup we are in. Mafia always knows which setup we are in based on whether they have a roleblocker.

It is possible that there is no doctor in the game. In this case, how is it possible that no one died night 1? There are two possibilities. Either the mafia was too inactive to submit a night actions (I confirmed with the moderator that the mafia mentor is not allowed to submit a night action for the player). Or, the mafia decided to make a gambit by intentionally not shooting anyone and then planning on claiming doctor later on, hoping that town would believe that no death => there is a doctor. Then, they can fakeclaim doctor and make everyone think they are confirmed town. It is a risky gambit, but it is one that a mafia player may make, seeing it as their only chance at winning the game when the odds are stacked against them.

If there is a doctor in the game, then mafia has access to a roleblocker, which they could have used to roleblock Johann and prevent the investigation from going through. However, we know for a fact that Johann was not roleblocked. Therefore, the only possibility in this case is that mafia must have decided to shoot Johann and roleblock someone they were sure was the doctor, rather than taking the safe route. This would require the last mafia member to have extremely high confidence in their guess for who doctor was, yet still managed to get it wrong. Then on night 2, they must have once again decided to once again attack johann and they roleblocked noodles, who they somehow determined was the doctor. Also note that in this scenario, Noodles must have been active enough to submit a protective action on night 1.

post_rendered: After some additional consideration, I realized that the mafia doing a gambit of trying to roleblock the doctor and shoot cop rather than roleblocking the cop and shooting the doctor on night 1 makes no sense as a plan, because doc can't heal themselves 2 nights in a row. So in the worst case if the doc big brained them and self healed night 1 and blocked your shot on them, the mafia can just shoot them again night 2 and continue roleblocking the cop. Doctor needs to successfully heal twice in order to meaningfully affect the balance of power, so roleblocking the cop is always the right move, even if you are very confident in who the doc is.
